purpose: Add a structured user-level preference prompt and a task/session-level instruction overlay to Ananta so users can persistently shape working style while still applying explicit task-specific constraints without bypassing governance.
goal: Users should be able to manage persistent personal operating preferences and separate task/session-specific instruction overlays in a controlled, visible and auditable way.
success_criteria: A persistent user profile prompt exists and can be managed safely.
A task or session instruction overlay exists separately from the persistent user profile.
The precedence between governance, blueprint/template logic, user profile and task overlay is explicit.
User-defined instructions cannot bypass governance, approval or security policy.
The resulting instruction stack is understandable, reviewable and auditable.

tasks.title: Instruction layer model define
acceptance_criteria: A clear layer model is defined for system/governance, blueprint or role template, user profile prompt, task/session overlay and current goal input.
The model is short enough to explain in docs and UI.
The model avoids one giant merged prompt concept.

tasks.title: Precedence and conflict rules define
acceptance_criteria: A precedence order is defined for all instruction layers.
Conflicts between user profile and task overlay are handled predictably.
Governance and security policy remain highest priority and cannot be overridden by user layers.

tasks.title: Allowed versus forbidden user influence scope define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines what user prompts may influence such as style, language, detail level or working mode.
The system defines what they may not override such as approval requirements, security policy and hard governance constraints.
The scope is explicit and testable.

tasks.title: Terminology cleanup decide
acceptance_criteria: A decision is made whether these features are named user profile prompt, preference profile, operating profile, task overlay or similar.
The chosen terminology is consistent across backend, UI, docs and API.
The naming reduces confusion with role templates or blueprint templates.

tasks.title: User profile prompt data model add
acceptance_criteria: A persistent user profile prompt model exists in the backend.
The model can store prompt content, metadata, owner and active/inactive state.
The model is compatible with future multi-profile support.

tasks.title: Multiple user profiles support design
acceptance_criteria: The design supports more than one profile per user such as coding, research or strict mode.
One profile can be marked default.
The design remains simple enough for first implementation.

tasks.title: CRUD API for user profiles implement
acceptance_criteria: Users can create, read, update and select their own profile prompts through an API.
Permissions prevent users from editing others' profile prompts unless explicitly allowed.
The API behavior is validated by tests.

tasks.title: Safe defaults for empty user profile define
acceptance_criteria: The system behaves predictably when no user profile prompt exists.
Empty profile state does not degrade into strange hidden defaults.
The default is documented.

tasks.title: Profile examples package add
acceptance_criteria: A few safe example profiles exist such as concise coding, research helper or review-first.
Examples do not encourage bypassing governance.
Examples help users understand the feature.

tasks.title: Task or session overlay data model add
acceptance_criteria: A distinct data model exists for a task/session/usage overlay.
The model is separate from the persistent user profile prompt.
The model can be attached to goal, task, session or explicit usage flow.

tasks.title: Overlay attachment points define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines which objects can carry an overlay such as goal, task, session or project context.
The attachment rules are explicit and avoid ambiguity.
The first release supports a practical subset of attachment points.

tasks.title: Overlay lifecycle define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines when an overlay begins, expires and stops applying.
The lifecycle distinguishes one-shot use, session use and project-scoped use.
Overlay lifetime is visible to the user.

tasks.title: CRUD and selection API for overlays implement
acceptance_criteria: The backend supports creating, reading, updating and attaching overlays.
The API clearly distinguishes overlays from user profiles.
The flow is testable and auditable.

tasks.title: Overlay visibility in task and goal read models add
acceptance_criteria: Relevant read models expose which overlay is active for a goal, task or session.
Users can see that an overlay is in effect.
The output is understandable and not too verbose.

tasks.title: Governance override prevention implement
acceptance_criteria: User profile prompts and task overlays cannot override approval, policy or hard safety constraints.
The enforcement is backend-side and not only UI-side.
Violations are blocked explicitly.

tasks.title: Prompt layer validation rules add
acceptance_criteria: The system validates profile prompts and overlays for forbidden directives where applicable.
Validation behavior is explicit and not surprising.
The validation can evolve without breaking the full model.

tasks.title: Audit integration for profile and overlay usage add
acceptance_criteria: Audit records show whether a user profile prompt or task overlay influenced an operation.
Audit output identifies which profile or overlay was active.
The feature supports later incident reconstruction.

tasks.title: Redaction strategy for stored prompts define
acceptance_criteria: Stored user profile prompts and overlays follow a redaction/storage policy where needed.
Sensitive content is handled safely in logs and audit views.
The policy is aligned with broader Ananta redaction behavior.

tasks.title: Policy conflict feedback UX define
acceptance_criteria: Users receive understandable feedback when their profile or overlay conflicts with governance.
The feedback is helpful rather than cryptic.
The UX does not imply that policy can simply be ignored.

tasks.title: Instruction assembly service implement
acceptance_criteria: A central service assembles the active instruction stack from all relevant layers.
Assembly does not happen ad hoc in many places.
The service is testable and explainable.

tasks.title: Layer merge strategy define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines whether layers are concatenated, normalized, structured or merged in another controlled way.
The merge strategy preserves governance precedence.
The result avoids chaotic prompt stacking.

tasks.title: Structured instruction rendering add
acceptance_criteria: The assembled instruction set can be represented in a structured internal form before final rendering.
The approach supports future explainability and diagnostics.
The structure remains practical.

tasks.title: Context-aware scope selection add
acceptance_criteria: The runtime can decide which user profile and which overlay apply in a given execution context.
The choice is deterministic and visible in diagnostics.
The logic handles missing or ambiguous context cleanly.

tasks.title: Instruction stack diagnostics add
acceptance_criteria: Operators or advanced users can inspect the effective instruction stack summary.
The diagnostics show which layers were applied and which were suppressed.
The output remains safe and readable.

tasks.title: User profile management UI add
acceptance_criteria: The UI allows users to create and manage their persistent profiles.
The UI makes profile purpose and active/default state clear.
The UI remains simple enough for real use.

tasks.title: Task overlay editor UI add
acceptance_criteria: Users can attach an overlay while creating or editing a goal/task/session where applicable.
The UI clearly distinguishes overlay from persistent user profile.
The active overlay is visible.

tasks.title: Effective instruction summary UI add
acceptance_criteria: The user can inspect a summary of the effective instruction stack before or during execution.
The summary is understandable without showing raw internals only.
The UI helps build trust.

tasks.title: Profile and overlay templates or presets add
acceptance_criteria: The UI can offer a few presets to accelerate adoption.
Presets are safe and aligned with product use cases.
Users can adapt them rather than starting from scratch every time.

tasks.title: First-run guidance for user profile prompts add
acceptance_criteria: New users get a lightweight explanation of the difference between profile and overlay.
The guidance helps avoid misuse.
The first-run experience stays compact.

tasks.title: Blueprint interaction rules define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines how user profiles and overlays interact with blueprints and role templates.
The rules avoid ambiguity between product-level defaults and user-level preferences.
The interaction model is documented.

tasks.title: Goal input schema integration add
acceptance_criteria: Relevant goal input flows can carry overlay or profile selection explicitly.
The schema remains usable and not bloated.
The change is reflected consistently in API and UI.

tasks.title: Role template compatibility checks add
acceptance_criteria: The system can detect when a user profile or overlay is incompatible with a role or template context.
The system can warn or block appropriately.
The behavior is understandable to admins and users.

tasks.title: Task and session reuse flows add
acceptance_criteria: Existing tasks or sessions can reuse an existing overlay where appropriate.
Reuse remains explicit rather than magical.
The feature supports recurring workflows.

tasks.title: Unit and contract tests for instruction precedence add
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove precedence and conflict rules across all instruction layers.
Governance override prevention is covered explicitly.
Regression risk is reduced.

tasks.title: Integration tests for profile plus overlay flows add
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end tests cover profile selection, overlay attachment and resulting execution behavior.
The tests verify that effective instructions are applied correctly.
The suite includes negative cases.

tasks.title: Authoring guide for user profiles and overlays write
acceptance_criteria: A guide explains how to write useful profile prompts and overlays.
The guide includes safe examples and anti-patterns.
The guide clearly explains that governance remains dominant.

tasks.title: Golden path example for persistent profile plus session overlay add
acceptance_criteria: There is one official example showing how a user profile and a task/session overlay work together.
The example is reproducible.
The example makes the feature tangible.

tasks.title: Migration and rollout plan define
acceptance_criteria: A rollout plan exists for introducing the feature without confusing current users.
The plan covers defaults, UI exposure and backward compatibility.
The plan avoids sudden hidden behavior changes.
